title: Aerdrie Faenya
summary: Elven goddess of air, weather, and avians, revered by those who dwell among the clouds and treetops.
content:
## Aerdrie Faenya

**Title:** The Winged Mother, Queen of the Avariel
**Alignment:** Chaotic Good
**Domains:** Tempest, Trickery
**Symbol:** A cloud with a bird silhouette
**Pantheon:** Seldarine

Aerdrie Faenya is the elven goddess of the sky, weather, and birds. She is the patron of the avariel (winged elves) and is beloved by all elves who live in high places or feel the call of open skies. Unpredictable and free-spirited, she delights in the beauty of storms and the wild majesty of high winds. Her followers often release captive birds as offerings, and her temples are built on mountaintops and tall trees where the wind blows freely.

Among the Seldarine, Aerdrie is known for her restless nature and her fierce independence. She rarely intervenes directly in mortal affairs, preferring to send omens through weather patterns and the flight of birds. Her clergy are loosely organized, valuing personal freedom over rigid hierarchy, and they serve as weather-readers, navigators, and protectors of avian creatures across the elven lands.

metadata_json.symbol: A cloud with a bird silhouette

title: Angharradh
summary: The triune elven goddess of wisdom, growth, and protection, formed from the union of three goddesses.
content:
## Angharradh

**Title:** The Triune Goddess, Queen of Arvandor
**Alignment:** Chaotic Good
**Domains:** Knowledge, Life
**Symbol:** Three interconnected circles
**Pantheon:** Seldarine

Angharradh is a unique deity among the Seldarine, representing the unified aspect of three elven goddesses: Aerdrie Faenya, Hanali Celanil, and Sehanine Moonbow. Worshipped primarily by moon elves and sun elves, she embodies the combined wisdom, love, and mystery of her three component aspects. She is considered by many to be the consort of Corellon Larethian and second in power among the elven pantheon.

Her worship emphasizes the strength found in unity and the interconnectedness of all aspects of elven life. Angharradh's clergy serve as advisors, healers, and protectors of elven communities, drawing on her threefold nature to address threats both physical and spiritual. Temples to Angharradh often feature tripartite architecture and gardens that grow through all seasons, symbolizing her domains of wisdom, growth, and protection.

metadata_json.symbol: Three interconnected circles

title: Deep Sashelas
summary: Elven god of the sea, creativity, and knowledge, patron of aquatic elves and sea-dwelling fey.
content:
## Deep Sashelas

**Title:** Lord of the Undersea, the Dolphin Prince, the Knowledgeable One
**Alignment:** Chaotic Good
**Domains:** Nature, Tempest
**Symbol:** A dolphin
**Pantheon:** Seldarine

Deep Sashelas is the elven god of oceans, seas, and marine life. He is the patron deity of aquatic elves (sea elves) and is revered by surface elves who live near the coast or make their living from the sea. Creative, passionate, and charismatic, Deep Sashelas is known for his love of art, music, and beauty in all its aquatic forms. His undersea realm is said to be filled with coral palaces and luminous gardens of breathtaking splendor.

Deep Sashelas maintains alliances with many good-aligned sea deities and actively opposes the sahuagin god Sekolah and other evil powers of the deep. His clergy are dedicated to protecting the seas from pollution and exploitation, fostering harmony between surface and aquatic elven communities, and preserving the creative arts. Temples to Deep Sashelas are often found in coastal grottos or submerged caverns, decorated with shells, pearls, and living coral.

metadata_json.symbol: A dolphin

title: Erevan Ilesere
summary: Elven god of mischief, change, and rogues, a trickster deity who delights in pranks and surprises.
content:
## Erevan Ilesere

**Title:** The Trickster, the Chameleon, the Green Changeling
**Alignment:** Chaotic Neutral
**Domains:** Trickery
**Symbol:** An asymmetrical starburst
**Pantheon:** Seldarine

Erevan Ilesere is the elven god of mischief, change, and cleverness. He is a consummate trickster who delights in turning the tables on the pompous, the arrogant, and the overly serious. Among the Seldarine, he serves as a reminder that life should never become too stale or predictable. His pranks range from harmless jests to elaborate schemes that reshape the fates of entire communities, though he rarely intends lasting harm.

Erevan's followers include rogues, bards, and any elf who embraces change and unpredictability. His clergy are informal and loosely organized, often operating as wandering tricksters who use humor and guile to expose corruption and challenge unjust authority. Temples to Erevan are rare and seldom stay in one place for long; his worship is more commonly practiced through acts of mischief, clever pranks, and the celebration of change and transformation.

metadata_json.symbol: An asymmetrical starburst

title: Fenmarel Mestarine
summary: Elven god of outcasts, solitude, and wild places, patron of those who live apart from elven society.
content:
## Fenmarel Mestarine

**Title:** The Lone Wolf, the One Apart
**Alignment:** Chaotic Neutral
**Domains:** Trickery
**Symbol:** A pair of elven eyes in darkness
**Pantheon:** Seldarine

Fenmarel Mestarine is the elven god of outcasts, scapegoats, and solitary elves who have been shunned or who have chosen to live apart from their communities. Brooding and withdrawn, he dwells on the fringes of the Seldarine, much as his followers dwell on the fringes of elven society. He teaches that solitude can be a source of strength and that survival in the wilderness depends on cunning, self-reliance, and wariness.

Despite his isolation, Fenmarel has not turned to evil. He harbors a deep, quiet love for the elven people and watches over those who have been cast out or forgotten. His small clergy consists of hermits, outcasts, and wild elves who patrol the borders between civilization and the untamed wilds. They serve as scouts, wardens, and guides for lost travelers, though they rarely seek thanks or recognition for their efforts.

metadata_json.symbol: A pair of elven eyes in darkness

title: Hanali Celanil
summary: Elven goddess of love, beauty, art, and enchantment, embodying romantic passion and aesthetic grace.
content:
## Hanali Celanil

**Title:** The Heart of Gold, Lady Goldheart, Winsome Rose
**Alignment:** Chaotic Good
**Domains:** Life
**Symbol:** A golden heart
**Pantheon:** Seldarine

Hanali Celanil is the elven goddess of romantic love, beauty, fine art, and enchantment. She is among the most beloved of the Seldarine, and her influence touches every elf who has ever known the joy of love or been moved by beauty. Graceful, warm, and endlessly compassionate, Hanali embodies the elven ideal of love as a force that enriches and ennobles all who experience it. Her golden pools in Arvandor are said to reflect the true beauty hidden in every heart.

Hanali's clergy promote love, beauty, and artistic expression throughout elven communities. They officiate weddings, counsel lovers, patronize artists, and maintain gardens and galleries dedicated to aesthetic perfection. Her temples are places of extraordinary beauty, filled with art, music, and the scent of flowers. Followers of Hanali believe that love is the greatest magic, more powerful than any spell, and that beauty in all its forms is a reflection of the divine.

metadata_json.symbol: A golden heart

title: Labelas Enoreth
summary: Elven god of time, history, and longevity, the patient philosopher who records the passage of ages.
content:
## Labelas Enoreth

**Title:** The Lifegiver, Lord of the Continuum, the Sage at Sunset
**Alignment:** Chaotic Good
**Domains:** Knowledge, Life
**Symbol:** A setting sun
**Pantheon:** Seldarine

Labelas Enoreth is the elven god of time, history, longevity, and memory. He is the patient chronicler of the Seldarine, recording the passage of ages and preserving the accumulated wisdom of elven civilization. Unlike many of his more impulsive kin, Labelas takes the long view, understanding that time reveals truths that haste obscures. He granted the elves their extraordinary longevity, and his blessings allow them to grow in wisdom throughout their centuries of life.

The clergy of Labelas are historians, sages, librarians, and teachers who dedicate their lives to preserving knowledge and passing it to future generations. They maintain great libraries and archives, record oral histories, and study the patterns of time to predict future events. Temples to Labelas are quiet, contemplative places filled with books, scrolls, and artifacts of ages past. His followers believe that understanding the past is the key to shaping a better future.

metadata_json.symbol: A setting sun

title: Rillifane Rallathil
summary: Elven god of nature, woodlands, and wild elves, manifest as a great tree whose roots connect all living things.
content:
## Rillifane Rallathil

**Title:** The Leaflord, the Wild One, the Great Oak
**Alignment:** Chaotic Good
**Domains:** Nature
**Symbol:** An oak tree
**Pantheon:** Seldarine

Rillifane Rallathil is the elven god of nature, forests, and the natural world. He is the patron of wood elves and wild elves, and his presence is felt in every ancient forest, every living tree, and every untamed wild place. Often depicted as a great green-skinned treant or an enormous oak tree, Rillifane embodies the interconnectedness of all living things through a vast network of roots and branches that spans the world.

Rillifane's clergy are druids, rangers, and woodland priests who serve as guardians of the forest and protectors of the natural world. They live simply, often dwelling in treehouses or forest clearings, and they oppose any who would despoil the wilderness through greed or carelessness. Temples to the Leaflord are groves of ancient trees, sometimes grown into living cathedrals of intertwined branches and leaves. His followers celebrate the changing seasons and teach that all creatures are part of a single great web of life.

metadata_json.symbol: An oak tree

title: Shevarash
summary: Elven god of vengeance, loss, and crusades against the drow, driven by unending grief and righteous fury.
content:
## Shevarash

**Title:** The Black Archer, the Night Hunter
**Alignment:** Chaotic Neutral
**Domains:** War
**Symbol:** A broken arrow over a tear
**Pantheon:** Seldarine

Shevarash is the elven god of vengeance, loss, and hatred of the drow. Once a mortal elf, he was elevated to divinity after swearing an oath of unending war against the dark elves following a devastating drow raid that killed his family. His rage has never diminished, and he is among the most grim and humorless of the Seldarine. He exists for a single purpose: the destruction of the drow and all who threaten the elven people.

Shevarash's followers are zealous warriors and hunters who dedicate their lives to fighting the drow and other enemies of the elven people. His clergy train for combat in the Underdark, study drow tactics and weaknesses, and lead raids against dark elf settlements. While other members of the Seldarine counsel patience and understanding, Shevarash demands action, sacrifice, and an unwavering commitment to the eradication of evil. His temples are austere armories and training grounds, devoid of the beauty common to other elven shrines.

metadata_json.symbol: A broken arrow over a tear

title: Solonor Thelandira
summary: Elven god of archery, hunting, and wilderness survival, the keen-eyed guardian of elven borders.
content:
## Solonor Thelandira

**Title:** Keen-Eye, the Great Archer, the Forest Hunter
**Alignment:** Chaotic Good
**Domains:** War
**Symbol:** A silver arrow with green fletching
**Pantheon:** Seldarine

Solonor Thelandira is the elven god of archery, hunting, and wilderness survival. He is the consummate hunter and archer, possessed of preternatural aim and an intimate understanding of the natural world. Among the Seldarine, he serves as the guardian of elven borders and the protector of those who make their living in the wild. His arrows are said to never miss their mark, and he can track any creature across any terrain.

Solonor's clergy are rangers, scouts, and hunters who patrol the borders of elven territories and protect their communities from external threats. They are masters of woodcraft, archery, and stealth, capable of surviving indefinitely in the deepest wilderness. Temples to Solonor are typically open-air shrines in forest clearings, marked by a quiver of silver arrows driven into the earth. His followers hold archery contests in his honor and teach that the hunt is a sacred act that connects the hunter to the primal rhythms of nature.

metadata_json.symbol: A silver arrow with green fletching

title: Eilistraee
summary: Drow goddess of song, beauty, swordwork, and freedom, who seeks to lead the dark elves back to the light.
content:
## Eilistraee

**Title:** The Dark Maiden, Lady of the Dance, Lady Silverhair
**Alignment:** Chaotic Good
**Domains:** Life, Light, Nature
**Symbol:** A nude long-haired female drow dancing with a silver bastard sword before a full moon
**Pantheon:** Dark Seldarine

Eilistraee is the drow goddess of song, swordwork, hunting, beauty, and moonlight. She is the only good-aligned deity of the Dark Seldarine, and she works tirelessly to redeem the drow and lead them away from the cruelty and darkness of Lolth's influence. Daughter of Corellon Larethian and formerly of the Seldarine, she chose exile alongside the drow out of love for them, believing she could guide them toward redemption from within.

Eilistraee's followers are drow who have rejected Lolth's tyranny and seek to live in peace with the surface world. Her clergy are warrior-priests who combine graceful swordplay with sacred dance and song, celebrating life, freedom, and beauty under the moonlight. They establish hidden sanctuaries where drow refugees can find safety and begin new lives on the surface. Despite facing hostility from both Lolth's faithful and surface-dwellers who distrust all drow, Eilistraee's faithful persist in their mission of reconciliation and hope.

metadata_json.symbol: A sword before a full moon

title: Ghaunadaur
summary: Drow god of oozes, slimes, and outcasts, an ancient and unpredictable entity of primordial chaos.
content:
## Ghaunadaur

**Title:** That Which Lurks, the Elder Eye, the Ancient One
**Alignment:** Chaotic Evil
**Domains:** War
**Symbol:** A purplish eye with a dark sclera
**Pantheon:** Dark Seldarine

Ghaunadaur is an ancient and terrifying deity worshipped by drow outcasts and those who traffic with oozes, slimes, and aberrations. Predating the Dark Seldarine itself, Ghaunadaur is a primordial entity of immense and alien power whose true nature defies mortal comprehension. He draws worshippers from among drow who have been marginalized by Lolth's priestesses, as well as other creatures of the Underdark who seek power through madness and dissolution.

Ghaunadaur's worship is unpredictable and dangerous. He may reward faithful servants with tremendous power or consume them on a whim, for his alien mind follows no logic that mortals can discern. His clergy perform rituals involving oozes, slimes, and other amorphous creatures, offering sacrifices to appease his inscrutable hunger. Temples to Ghaunadaur are hidden deep in the Underdark, built around pools of living slime, and his followers are feared and shunned even by other drow for their devotion to such an incomprehensible power.

metadata_json.symbol: A purplish eye with a dark sclera

title: Kiaransalee
summary: Drow goddess of the undead and vengeance, a bitter deity who commands legions of revenants and wraiths.
content:
## Kiaransalee

**Title:** The Revenancer, the Vengeful Banshee, Lady of the Dead
**Alignment:** Chaotic Evil
**Domains:** Death
**Symbol:** A female drow hand wearing silver rings
**Pantheon:** Dark Seldarine

Kiaransalee is the drow goddess of undead and vengeance. Once a mortal drow queen of extraordinary necromantic power, she achieved divinity through sheer force of will and an unquenchable thirst for revenge against those who wronged her. She commands vast legions of undead and teaches her followers that death is not an end but a tool to be wielded against one's enemies. Her bitterness knows no bounds, and she nurses grudges across millennia.

Kiaransalee's clergy are necromancers and death priests who raise and command undead in service to their goddess. They maintain hidden temples filled with animated corpses and spectral servants, and they plot elaborate schemes of vengeance against perceived enemies. Her worship is suppressed even in drow society, as Lolth views the Revenancer as a dangerous rival. Despite this persecution, Kiaransalee's cult persists in the darkest corners of the Underdark, sustained by the promise of power over death and the sweetness of revenge.

metadata_json.symbol: A female drow hand wearing silver rings

title: Selvetarm
summary: Drow god of warriors and savagery, a fierce berserker enslaved to Lolth's will through treachery.
content:
## Selvetarm

**Title:** Champion of Lolth, the Spider that Waits, Prince of the Aranea
**Alignment:** Chaotic Evil
**Domains:** War
**Symbol:** A spider over a crossed sword and mace
**Pantheon:** Dark Seldarine

Selvetarm is the drow god of warriors, battle prowess, and savagery. He is the patron of drow fighters who revel in combat and bloodshed, offering them strength and ferocity in battle. Originally the son of drow gods who sought his own path, Selvetarm was tricked by Lolth into slaying a demon and absorbing its essence, which drove him mad and bound him to Lolth's service. He now serves as her unwilling champion, consumed by rage at his enslavement but unable to break free.

Selvetarm's followers are elite drow warriors who channel their fury into devastating combat prowess. His clergy, predominantly male, train obsessively in the fighting arts and seek glory through martial excellence. They serve as shock troops, champions, and enforcers within drow society. Temples to Selvetarm are spartan fighting pits where warriors train endlessly, spilling blood in his name. Despite his hatred of his servitude to Lolth, Selvetarm's followers dutifully serve the Spider Queen's agenda, for their god's rage only fuels their own destructive power.

metadata_json.symbol: A spider over a crossed sword and mace

title: Vhaeraun
summary: Drow god of thievery, intrigue, and male drow, who plots to overthrow Lolth's matriarchal tyranny.
content:
## Vhaeraun

**Title:** The Masked Lord, the Masked God of Night, the Shadow
**Alignment:** Chaotic Evil
**Domains:** Trickery, War
**Symbol:** A black mask with blue glass lenses
**Pantheon:** Dark Seldarine

Vhaeraun is the drow god of thievery, intrigue, and male drow who chafe under the matriarchal dominance of Lolth's priestesses. Son of Corellon and Lolth, he was banished alongside his mother during the elven schism but has never accepted her authority. Cunning, ambitious, and patient, Vhaeraun works to undermine Lolth's power and establish a drow society where males hold equal standing. He also encourages drow expansion onto the surface world through stealth and subterfuge rather than open warfare.

Vhaeraun's clergy are exclusively male, operating in secret cells and shadow networks that infiltrate drow society at every level. They are master thieves, assassins, and spies who work to subvert Lolth's priestesses and advance their god's agenda of equality and surface expansion. His followers wear distinctive black half-masks and communicate through elaborate codes and signs. Despite being hunted by Lolth's faithful, Vhaeraun's cult thrives in the shadows, fueled by the resentment of male drow who dream of freedom from the Spider Queen's oppressive rule.

metadata_json.symbol: A black mask with blue glass lenses

title: Abbathor
summary: Dwarven god of greed and treasure, the avaricious member of the Morndinsamman whose hunger for gold knows no limit.
content:
## Abbathor

**Title:** The Great Master of Greed, Trove Lord, the Avaricious
**Alignment:** Neutral Evil
**Domains:** Trickery
**Symbol:** A jeweled dagger
**Pantheon:** Morndinsamman

Abbathor is the dwarven god of greed, avarice, and treasure. He is the black sheep of the Morndinsamman, tolerated but not trusted by the other dwarven deities. Where Moradin teaches that wealth comes through honest labor and craftsmanship, Abbathor whispers that treasure is meant to be seized, hoarded, and guarded jealously. His influence is felt whenever a dwarf places gold above honor, or when the lure of treasure leads a mining expedition deeper than wisdom permits.

Despite his evil alignment, Abbathor is not entirely shunned in dwarven society. Many dwarves offer him small prayers before risky ventures, hoping his blessing will lead them to rich veins of ore or forgotten treasure hoards. His clergy operate discreetly, serving as appraisers, treasure-hunters, and sometimes thieves. They maintain hidden vaults filled with stolen wealth and plot to acquire ever more riches. Moradin tolerates Abbathor's presence in the pantheon because greed, channeled properly, drives dwarves to dig deeper and craft finer works.

metadata_json.symbol: A jeweled dagger

title: Berronar Truesilver
summary: Dwarven goddess of hearth, home, and truth, the matriarch of the Morndinsamman and consort of Moradin.
content:
## Berronar Truesilver

**Title:** The Revered Mother, Mother of Safety, the Faithful
**Alignment:** Lawful Good
**Domains:** Life, Light
**Symbol:** Two intertwined silver rings
**Pantheon:** Morndinsamman

Berronar Truesilver is the dwarven goddess of hearth, home, truth, and family. She is the consort of Moradin and the matriarch of the Morndinsamman, beloved by all dwarves as the protector of family bonds, keeper of records, and guardian of the home. Wise, patient, and fiercely protective of her charges, Berronar embodies the strength and warmth at the heart of every dwarven household.

Berronar's clergy are healers, counselors, judges, and record-keepers who serve as the moral backbone of dwarven communities. They officiate marriages, mediate disputes, tend the sick and wounded, and maintain the genealogical records that are so vital to dwarven identity. Temples to Berronar are warm, welcoming places that serve as community centers, hospitals, and archives. Her followers swear oaths of honesty and devotion to family, and they are among the most respected members of any dwarven settlement.

metadata_json.symbol: Two intertwined silver rings

title: Clangeddin Silverbeard
summary: Dwarven god of war, valor, and battle, the fearless warrior who leads dwarven armies against their foes.
content:
## Clangeddin Silverbeard

**Title:** The Father of Battle, Lord of the Twin Axes, the Rock of Battle
**Alignment:** Lawful Good
**Domains:** War
**Symbol:** Two crossed battle-axes
**Pantheon:** Morndinsamman

Clangeddin Silverbeard is the dwarven god of battle, war, valor, and honor in combat. He is the supreme warrior of the Morndinsamman, a fearless and indomitable fighter who has never retreated from battle. Clangeddin despises cowardice, treachery, and those who use underhanded tactics in warfare. He teaches that combat is the ultimate test of a dwarf's character, and that courage in battle is the highest virtue.

Clangeddin's clergy are warrior-priests who serve as military commanders, battle chaplains, and weapons instructors in dwarven strongholds. They train constantly, maintain the martial readiness of their communities, and lead dwarven forces against giants, orcs, goblinoids, and other ancestral enemies. Temples to Clangeddin are combination armories and war shrines, decorated with the weapons and banners of defeated foes. His followers believe that a dwarf who dies bravely in battle earns a place of honor in the halls of the Morndinsamman.

metadata_json.symbol: Two crossed battle-axes

title: Dugmaren Brightmantle
summary: Dwarven god of scholarship, discovery, and invention, the curious tinkerer who seeks knowledge for its own sake.
content:
## Dugmaren Brightmantle

**Title:** The Gleam in the Eye, the Errant Explorer, the Wandering Tinker
**Alignment:** Chaotic Good
**Domains:** Knowledge
**Symbol:** An open book
**Pantheon:** Morndinsamman

Dugmaren Brightmantle is the dwarven god of scholarship, discovery, and invention. He is the most unconventional of the Morndinsamman, constantly seeking new knowledge, experimenting with unusual ideas, and questioning established traditions. Where most dwarven deities value stability and tradition, Dugmaren embraces curiosity and change, believing that progress comes through questioning and exploration rather than adherence to the old ways.

Dugmaren's clergy are scholars, inventors, sages, and alchemists who pursue knowledge in all its forms. They maintain libraries and workshops where they conduct experiments, study ancient texts, and develop new tools and techniques. Temples to Dugmaren are part laboratory, part library, and part museum, filled with curiosities collected from across the world. His followers are sometimes viewed with suspicion by more conservative dwarves, but their discoveries and inventions frequently prove invaluable to their communities.

metadata_json.symbol: An open book

title: Dumathoin
summary: Dwarven god of buried secrets, mining, and exploration, the silent keeper of treasures hidden beneath the earth.
content:
## Dumathoin

**Title:** Keeper of Secrets Under the Mountain, the Silent Keeper, the Mountain Shield
**Alignment:** Neutral
**Domains:** Death, Knowledge
**Symbol:** A cut gemstone inside a mountain
**Pantheon:** Morndinsamman

Dumathoin is the dwarven god of buried secrets, mining, gems, and exploration of the underground world. He is the patron of miners, gemcutters, and all who delve into the earth seeking its hidden riches. Silent and contemplative, Dumathoin rarely speaks, even to other deities, preferring to communicate through the placement of mineral veins and the revelation of hidden caverns. He placed precious metals and gems within the earth as gifts for the dwarven people, and he watches over those who seek them.

Dumathoin also serves as the guardian of the dwarven dead, protecting the secrets of those who have passed on and watching over burial sites. His clergy are miners, gemcutters, and funerary priests who tend both the living miners and the honored dead. Temples to Dumathoin are carved deep within mountains, often adjacent to rich mining galleries, and they serve as both places of worship and final resting places. His followers take vows of silence during their devotions and believe that the greatest treasures are those that remain hidden, waiting to be discovered by the worthy.

metadata_json.symbol: A cut gemstone inside a mountain

title: Gorm Gulthyn
summary: Dwarven god of vigilance and defense, the tireless sentinel who guards the gates of dwarven strongholds.
content:
## Gorm Gulthyn

**Title:** Fire Eyes, the Golden Guardian, Lord of the Bronze Mask, the Eternally Vigilant
**Alignment:** Lawful Good
**Domains:** War
**Symbol:** A bronze half-mask
**Pantheon:** Morndinsamman

Gorm Gulthyn is the dwarven god of vigilance, defense, and guardianship. He is the eternal sentinel of the Morndinsamman, standing watch at the gates of the divine realm just as his followers stand watch at the gates of dwarven strongholds. His eyes burn with golden fire, seeing through deception and piercing the darkness, and he never sleeps, never falters, and never abandons his post. He is the embodiment of the dwarven commitment to protecting hearth and home.

Gorm's clergy are guards, watchmen, and defensive specialists who dedicate their lives to protecting dwarven settlements from attack. They organize watch schedules, maintain fortifications, train sentries, and develop defensive strategies against the many threats that lurk in the depths. Temples to Gorm are built into the walls and gatehouses of dwarven fortresses, designed to be easily defensible and to serve as rallying points during sieges. His followers wear bronze half-masks in emulation of their god and take sacred oaths never to abandon their posts.

metadata_json.symbol: A bronze half-mask

title: Haela Brightaxe
summary: Dwarven goddess of luck in battle, joyful combat, and the thrill of fighting against overwhelming odds.
content:
## Haela Brightaxe

**Title:** Lady of the Fray, the Luckmaiden
**Alignment:** Chaotic Good
**Domains:** War
**Symbol:** An upright sword with a blood drop on the blade
**Pantheon:** Morndinsamman

Haela Brightaxe is the dwarven goddess of luck in battle, joyful combat, and the exhilaration of fighting against overwhelming odds. She is the most exuberant and spontaneous of the Morndinsamman, delighting in the chaos and excitement of melee combat. Unlike the disciplined Clangeddin, Haela embraces the unpredictable nature of battle, trusting in luck, instinct, and sheer enthusiasm to carry the day. She especially favors those who fight bravely against giants and other large creatures.

Haela's followers are berserkers, adventurers, and warriors who live for the thrill of combat. Her clergy lead charges against seemingly impossible odds, trusting in their goddess's luck to see them through. They celebrate victories with boisterous feasts and retell tales of unlikely triumphs. Temples to Haela are informal gathering halls where warriors share battle stories and pledge themselves to glorious combat. Her followers believe that fortune favors the bold, and that a dwarf who fights with joy in their heart will always find luck on their side.

metadata_json.symbol: An upright sword with a blood drop on the blade

title: Marthammor Duin
summary: Dwarven god of wanderers, travelers, and lightning, the patron of dwarves who explore the world above.
content:
## Marthammor Duin

**Title:** Finder-of-Trails, Watcher Over Wanderers, the Watchful Eye
**Alignment:** Neutral Good
**Domains:** Nature, Trickery
**Symbol:** An upright mace before a tall boot
**Pantheon:** Morndinsamman

Marthammor Duin is the dwarven god of wanderers, travelers, and those who explore the surface world. While most dwarven deities are concerned with life underground, Marthammor encourages dwarves to venture beyond their mountain halls, explore new lands, and establish connections with other races. He is the patron of dwarven merchants, diplomats, and adventurers who travel the roads and trails of the surface world.

Marthammor's clergy are guides, trail-blazers, and protectors of travelers. They maintain way-stations and shelters along trade routes, provide directions and hospitality to lost travelers, and scout new territories for possible dwarven expansion. Temples to Marthammor are often roadside shrines or traveler's rest stops rather than grand underground halls. His followers carry maps and trail markers, and they believe that the world beyond the mountain holds wonders worth discovering. Among the Morndinsamman, Marthammor serves as a bridge between the insular dwarven world and the wider civilizations of the surface.

metadata_json.symbol: An upright mace before a tall boot

title: Vergadain
summary: Dwarven god of wealth, luck, and chance, the shrewd negotiator and patron of dwarven merchants.
content:
## Vergadain

**Title:** The Merchant King, Trickster Among the Morndinsamman, the Short Father
**Alignment:** Neutral
**Domains:** Trickery
**Symbol:** A gold coin bearing a dwarven face
**Pantheon:** Morndinsamman

Vergadain is the dwarven god of wealth, luck, chance, and commerce. He is the patron of dwarven merchants, traders, and negotiators, and he represents the more light-hearted and cunning aspects of dwarven culture. Unlike Abbathor, whose greed is purely selfish, Vergadain values the art of the deal, the thrill of a gamble, and the satisfaction of a fair trade that benefits both parties. He is charming, witty, and always looking for an opportunity to profit.

Vergadain's clergy are merchants, traders, bankers, and gamblers who use their wits and charm to generate prosperity for their communities. They negotiate trade agreements, manage dwarven treasuries, and run the markets and trading posts that connect dwarven settlements to the wider world. Temples to Vergadain are often combined with marketplaces and trading halls, and they feature vaults for storing community wealth. His followers believe that wealth honestly earned through clever dealing is a blessing to the entire clan, and they celebrate luck as a divine gift to be embraced and tested.

metadata_json.symbol: A gold coin bearing a dwarven face
